title: Trigger retraining from drift, not the calendar
description: Drive retraining from measured input and label drift signals rather than a fixed schedule, so refresh tracks real distribution change.
body: Use when deciding how often a production model should be refreshed. Avoid a pure calendar cron — it retrains when nothing changed and stays stale when the world shifts fast. Actions: monitor feature drift (PSI or KS) and label/outcome drift, set thresholds, and fire the retrain pipeline when a signal crosses; record which signal triggered each run. Validation signals include a drift dashboard with thresholds and a retrain log linking each run to the breaching metric. Risk boundary: retraining on drifted-but-corrupt data poisons the model; if drift coincides with an upstream data-quality alert, halt the trigger and escalate to a human.
